steps.title: Write the volume comparison
steps.content:
A cylinder's volume is

$$V=\pi r^2h$$

If the second cylinder has radius $R$ and height $H$, its scale factor compared with the first is

$$\frac{\pi R^2H}{\pi r^2h}$$

steps.title: Factor the required multiplier
steps.content:
The volume must be $392$ times as large:

$$392=49\cdot 8=7^2\cdot 8$$

A radius multiplier of $7$ contributes $7^2=49$, and a height multiplier of $8$ contributes the remaining factor.

steps.title: Choose the matching dimensions
steps.content:
The dimensions $R=7r$ and $H=8h$ give

$$\pi(7r)^2(8h)=392\pi r^2h$$

The answer is C.
